blurb: Enhancement Shaman is arena's lottery melee: one double-Windfury proc can end a game on the spot. Between procs you bring the full totem toolkit — Grounding, Tremor, Earthbind — and shocks for interrupts. Ladder enhancement shamans gear defensively because everyone kites them, and the windfury math works fine off PvP weapons.
statPriorityRationale: Enhancement's windfury rolls are the biggest RNG burst in the game — the gear's job is to keep you alive long enough to fish for them, so survivability leads and attack power sharpens the payoff.

faq.question: What rating are these Enhancement Shamans?
faq.answer: This snapshot covers 10 Enhancement Shamans between 2136 and 2817 rating (average 2319) on the TBC Classic arena ladder, refreshed from public leaderboard data.

faq.question: What is the most-used Enhancement Shaman PvP gear right now?
faq.answer: The highest-consensus picks are Totem of the Astral Winds (100%), Veteran's Pendant of Triumph (90%), Veteran's Band of Triumph (90%). Anything above ~70% usage at this rating range is effectively mandatory.

faq.question: How much of the Enhancement Shaman BiS list is PvP gear vs raid gear?
faq.answer: 80% of the most-used slots are arena/honor items — for Enhancement Shamans the resilience on PvP gear beats raid alternatives almost everywhere. The exceptions are listed as alternatives per slot.

faq.question: What weapon do Enhancement Shamans use in arena?
faq.answer: Rod of the Sun King is the main-hand of choice (30% of surveyed players).

faq.question: What gems do Enhancement Shamans socket for PvP?
faq.answer: Across all sockets, the most-used gems in this snapshot are +8 Strength, +8 Resilience Rating, +10 Attack Power, +5 Critical Strike Rating. Socket bonuses are usually ignored unless they align with these.
